User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the IIIT Dharwad AIS platform, capable of being a Professor or a Student. Authentication handled externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Professor', 'Student').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an academic course offered on the platform, managed by a professor, and enrolled by students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

professorId.description: Reference to the User entity who is the professor teaching this course. (Relationship: User 1:N Course)

joinCode.description: An auto-generated unique code that students can use to enroll in the course.

semester.description: The academic semester in which the course is offered (e.g., 'Fall 2023').

isActive.description: Indicates whether the course is currently active, open for enrollment, or accepting submissions.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

CourseEnrollment.title: CourseEnrollment
CourseEnrollment.description: Represents a student's enrollment in a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseEnrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User entity who is the student enrolled in the course. (Relationship: User 1:N CourseEnrollment)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course entity the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Course 1:N CourseEnrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: Timestamp when the student enrolled in the course.

isArchived.description: Indicates if the course enrollment is archived, typically after the course concludes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the enrollment record was last updated.

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Represents an academic assignment created by a professor within a course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course entity this assignment belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Assignment)

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment, including instructions and requirements.

deadline.description: The submission deadline for the assignment.

allowResubmissions.description: Indicates whether students are allowed to resubmit the assignment.

maxResubmissions.description: The maximum number of times a student can resubmit the assignment, if allowed.

enableLeaderboard.description: Indicates whether a leaderboard for this assignment should be enabled.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the assignment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the assignment was last updated.

Rubric.title: Rubric
Rubric.description: Defines the grading criteria for an assignment, generated by AI and refined by professors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rubric entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this rubric is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:1 Rubric)

name.description: A descriptive name for the rubric (e.g., 'AI-Generated Rubric for Essay 1').

description.description: A general description of the rubric's purpose or scope.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rubric was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the rubric was last updated.

RubricCriterion.title: RubricCriterion
RubricCriterion.description: A single criterion within a rubric, defining a specific aspect for evaluation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RubricCriterion entity.

rubricId.description: Reference to the Rubric entity this criterion belongs to. (Relationship: Rubric 1:N RubricCriterion)

description.description: A description of the specific evaluation criterion (e.g., 'Clarity of Argument').

maxScore.description: The maximum score attainable for this criterion.

order.description: The display order of this criterion within its rubric.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rubric criterion was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the rubric criterion was last updated.

ProjectGroup.title: ProjectGroup
ProjectGroup.description: Represents a group of students collaborating on a group project for a specific assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProjectGroup entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course entity the group project is associated with. (Relationship: Course 1:N ProjectGroup)

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this group is formed for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N ProjectGroup)

name.description: The name of the project group (e.g., 'Team Alpha').

memberIds.description: References to User entities who are members of this group. (Relationship: User N:N ProjectGroup)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the project group was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the project group was last updated.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Represents a student's (or group's) attempt to submit an assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Submission entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this submission is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Submission)

submitterId.description: Reference to the User entity who submitted the assignment (individual or lead for group). (Relationship: User 1:N Submission)

groupId.description: Optional reference to the ProjectGroup entity if this is a group submission. (Relationship: ProjectGroup 1:N Submission)

submissionNumber.description: The sequential number of this submission for a given assignment and student/group (e.g., 1 for first submission, 2 for first resubmission).

submissionType.description: The format of the submitted content (e.g., 'text', 'file', 'zip', 'github', 'drive').

content.description: The actual submitted content (e.g., text, file path/URL, GitHub repository URL, Google Drive link).

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the submission was made.

isLate.description: Indicates whether the submission was made after the assignment deadline.

aiQualityWarning.description: An AI-generated warning or feedback regarding submission quality prior to final submission.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the submission record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the submission record was last updated.

Evaluation.title: Evaluation
Evaluation.description: Stores the AI-driven assessment results and personalized feedback for a specific student or group submission.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Evaluation entity.

submissionId.description: Reference to the Submission entity that this evaluation is for. (Relationship: Submission 1:1 Evaluation)

totalScore.description: The overall score for the submission, as initially determined by AI.

professorOverrideScore.description: The score set by the professor, if they override the AI-generated total score.

aiConfidenceScore.description: A score (0-1) indicating the AI's confidence in its evaluation.

writtenFeedback.description: AI-generated comprehensive written feedback for the submission.

weakAreasDetected.description: A list of identified weak areas in the submission, detected by AI.

plagiarismFlagged.description: Indicates whether plagiarism was detected in the submission.

plagiarismDetails.description: Details about the detected plagiarism, if any.

gitHubAnalysis.description: Detailed AI analysis for GitHub repository submissions (e.g., code quality, commit history).

resubmissionComparison.description: AI analysis comparing the current submission with previous resubmissions, highlighting improvements or regressions.

learningResourceIds.description: References to LearningResource entities suggested by AI based on identified weak areas. (Relationship: Evaluation N:N LearningResource)

groupEvaluationData.description: JSON string containing group-specific evaluation data, such as commit history analysis, cross-checked contribution reports, and flagged conflicts for professor review.

evaluatedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI evaluation was completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the evaluation record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the evaluation record was last updated.

EvaluationCriterionScore.title: EvaluationCriterionScore
EvaluationCriterionScore.description: Stores the individual score for each rubric criterion as part of an overall evaluation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EvaluationCriterionScore entity.

evaluationId.description: Reference to the Evaluation entity this criterion score belongs to. (Relationship: Evaluation 1:N EvaluationCriterionScore)

rubricCriterionId.description: Reference to the RubricCriterion entity that this score corresponds to. (Relationship: RubricCriterion 1:N EvaluationCriterionScore)

score.description: The score achieved for this specific rubric criterion.

feedback.description: Specific feedback pertaining to this rubric criterion.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the criterion score record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the criterion score record was last updated.

IndividualGroupMemberScore.title: IndividualGroupMemberScore
IndividualGroupMemberScore.description: Stores the individual score for a student participating in a group project, as determined by AI and/or professor, based on the group's overall evaluation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IndividualGroupMemberScore entity.

evaluationId.description: Reference to the Evaluation entity for the group submission. (Relationship: Evaluation 1:N IndividualGroupMemberScore)

studentId.description: Reference to the User entity for the specific student in the group. (Relationship: User 1:N IndividualGroupMemberScore)

score.description: The individual score assigned to this student for the group project.

feedback.description: Specific feedback for the individual student's contribution to the group project.

isConflicted.description: Indicates if AI flagged a conflict regarding this student's contribution or score, requiring professor review.

professorOverrideScore.description: The individual score set by the professor, if they override the AI-generated individual score.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the individual score record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the individual score record was last updated.

GroupContributionReport.title: GroupContributionReport
GroupContributionReport.description: Report submitted by students or professor regarding individual contributions to a group project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GroupContributionReport entity.

groupId.description: Reference to the ProjectGroup entity this report is for. (Relationship: ProjectGroup 1:N GroupContributionReport)

submissionId.description: Reference to the Submission entity of the group project this report is associated with. (Relationship: Submission 1:N GroupContributionReport)

reportingStudentId.description: Reference to the User entity who submitted this contribution report. (Relationship: User 1:N GroupContributionReport)

reportedStudentId.description: Reference to the User entity whose contribution is being reported on. (Relationship: User 1:N GroupContributionReport)

contributionDetails.description: Narrative description of the reported student's contribution to the project.

contributionScore.description: A score or rating reflecting the reported student's contribution, often from self/peer assessment.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the contribution report was submitted.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the contribution report record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the contribution report record was last updated.

LearningResource.title: LearningResource
LearningResource.description: Represents a suggested learning resource for students, often provided by AI based on weak areas.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningResource entity.

title.description: The title of the learning resource.

description.description: A brief description of what the resource covers.

url.description: The URL to access the learning resource.

keywords.description: Keywords or tags associated with the resource for categorization and search.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the learning resource was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the learning resource was last updated.

CourseContent.title: CourseContent
CourseContent.description: Represents various types of content shared by a professor within a course, including announcements, files, links, and embedded videos.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseContent entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course entity this content belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N CourseContent)

professorId.description: Reference to the User entity who posted this content. (Relationship: User 1:N CourseContent)

title.description: The title of the content item.

content.description: The main content (e.g., text for announcement, URL for link/video, file reference).

attachmentUrl.description: Optional URL for an attached file, if applicable (e.g., PDF, document, ZIP).

postedAt.description: Timestamp when the content was posted.

isPinned.description: Indicates if the content (e.g., announcement) is pinned for prominent display.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the content record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the content record was last updated.

PrivateMessage.title: PrivateMessage
PrivateMessage.description: Represents a private message sent between a student and a professor within the system, potentially linked to a course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PrivateMessage entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course entity this message is associated with, if applicable. (Relationship: Course 1:N PrivateMessage)

senderId.description: Reference to the User entity who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N PrivateMessage)

receiverId.description: Reference to the User entity who is the recipient of the message. (Relationship: User 1:N PrivateMessage)

subject.description: The subject line of the private message.

body.description: The main content of the private message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the recipient has read the message.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the private message record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the private message record was last updated.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records significant actions and changes within the system for audit purposes, especially professor overrides.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entity.

actorId.description: Reference to the User entity who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

entityType.description: The type of entity that was affected by the action (e.g., 'Evaluation', 'Course', 'Assignment').

entityId.description: The unique identifier of the entity that was affected.

oldValue.description: The previous state or value of the affected data, represented as a JSON string.

newValue.description: The new state or value of the affected data, represented as a JSON string.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the audit event occurred.

description.description: A human-readable description of the action and its context.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the audit log record was created.

StudentPortfolio.title: StudentPortfolio
StudentPortfolio.description: An automatically generated personal portfolio for a student, displaying their academic progress, assignments, and scores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentPortfolio entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User entity this portfolio belongs to. (Relationship: User 1:1 StudentPortfolio)

title.description: The title of the student's portfolio (e.g., 'John Doe's Academic Portfolio').

shareableLink.description: A publicly accessible URL for sharing the student's portfolio.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio was initially generated.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the portfolio content was last automatically updated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the portfolio record was last updated.

ChatbotInteraction.title: ChatbotInteraction
ChatbotInteraction.description: Stores interactions with the AI Chatbot Assistant for context, history, and analytics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatbotInteraction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity who interacted with the chatbot. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatbotInteraction)

query.description: The user's question or prompt submitted to the chatbot.

response.description: The chatbot's generated response to the user's query.

contextData.description: JSON string containing relevant contextual data used by the chatbot during this interaction (e.g., 'courseId', 'assignmentId').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chatbot interaction record was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access is typically restricted to the user themselves (read/write) or authorized administrators/professors (read-only for specific fields). The 'role' field is mirrored in dedicated role collections for atomic access control.
Sentinel collection. The existence of a document here (even empty) grants 'professor' role permissions to the '{userId}'. Used for atomic DBAC checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user with the professor role.

definition.description: Sentinel collection. The existence of a document here (even empty) grants 'student' role permissions to the '{userId}'. Used for atomic DBAC checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user with the student role.

definition.description: Top-level collection for academic courses. Includes denormalized 'professorId' for authorization independence and direct ownership checks.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the course.

definition.description: Top-level collection for student course enrollments. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) and 'studentId' for authorization independence, allowing direct checks for both professors and students.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the course enrollment record.

definition.description: Assignments within a specific course. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the assignment.

definition.description: Rubrics associated with a specific assignment. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: rubricId
params.description: The unique ID of the rubric.

definition.description: Individual criteria within a rubric. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: rubricId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent rubric.

params.name: criterionId
params.description: The unique ID of the rubric criterion.

definition.description: Project groups formed for assignments within a course. Includes 'memberIds' array for student access and denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) for professor access and authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique ID of the project group.

definition.description: Submissions for assignments. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) and 'submitterId' for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the submission.

definition.description: AI evaluations for submissions. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) and 'submitterId' (from Submission) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent submission.

params.name: evaluationId
params.description: The unique ID of the evaluation.

definition.description: Individual scores per rubric criterion within an evaluation. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) and 'submitterId' (from Submission) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent submission.

params.name: evaluationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent evaluation.

params.name: scoreId
params.description: The unique ID for the criterion score.

definition.description: Individual scores for group members within a group evaluation. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course), 'submitterId' (from Submission for the group), and 'studentId' (the individual student) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent group submission.

params.name: evaluationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent evaluation.

params.name: individualScoreId
params.description: The unique ID for the individual group member score.

definition.description: Contribution reports for group projects. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course), 'groupId', 'reportingStudentId', and 'reportedStudentId' for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent group submission.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique ID for the group contribution report.

definition.description: Global collection for AI-suggested learning resources. Generally readable by all authenticated users.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique ID of the learning resource.

definition.description: Various content types (announcements, files, links) posted within a course by professors. Includes denormalized 'professorId' (from Course) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent course.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique ID of the course content item.

definition.description: Private messages between users. Includes 'senderId' and 'receiverId' for direct authorization checks by either party.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the private message.

definition.description: Records significant actions and overrides. Primarily accessible by professors for auditing purposes. Includes 'actorId' for logging.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique ID of the audit log entry.

definition.description: Student's personal academic portfolio, owned by the student. Access is restricted to the student and potentially professors for courses they teach.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the student user.

params.name: portfolioId
params.description: The unique ID of the student's portfolio.

definition.description: Records interactions with the AI Chatbot Assistant, owned by the user who interacted. Access is restricted to the user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who interacted with the chatbot.

params.name: interactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the chatbot interaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy for Authorization Independence is **extensive denormalization of authorization context**. Specifically, for any subcollection or related collection whose access depends on a 'parent' entity's ownership or membership, the relevant identifier (e.g., `professorId`, `studentId`, `memberIds`) is explicitly copied into the document itself. For instance, `Course` documents contain `professorId`, and crucially, `Assignment`, `Rubric`, `RubricCriterion`, `ProjectGroup`, `CourseContent`, `Submission`, `Evaluation`, `EvaluationCriterionScore`, `IndividualGroupMemberScore`, and `GroupContributionReport` documents all denormalize `professorId` from their respective parent `Course` entity. This means that security rules evaluating access for these documents do not need to perform `get()` operations on parent collections, enabling atomic transactions and preventing complex, hard-to-debug rule dependencies.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by ensuring that all documents within a given collection path share a homogeneous security posture. For example, `Course` documents are managed by their respective professors, while `CourseEnrollment` documents track student membership. User-specific data, such as `StudentPortfolio` and `ChatbotInteraction`, is organized under a `/users/{userId}/...` path, enforcing strict path-based ownership. Global entities like `LearningResource` are placed in top-level collections with broader read access.

**Access Modeling** utilizes standard patterns:
1.  **Private Data:** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/portfolio/{portfolioId}` and `/users/{userId}/chatbot_interactions/{interactionId}`) is used for inherently private user data, where `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`.
2.  **Hierarchical Paths:** Most entities follow a logical hierarchy (e.g., `/courses/{courseId}/assignments/{assignmentId}/submissions/{submissionId}`) to reflect their natural relationships. Each document in these nested paths includes necessary denormalized `professorId` (and `submitterId` for submissions/evaluations) to allow direct authorization checks without costly lookups.
3.  **Collaborative Data:** `ProjectGroup` documents include a `memberIds` array, allowing security rules to efficiently check if `request.auth.uid` is part of the group for access control.
4.  **Global Roles (DBAC):** Instead of relying on a `get()` call to the `User` document to fetch the `role` field, dedicated collections `/professor_roles/{userId}` and `/student_roles/{userId}` are used. The *existence* of a document at these paths signifies the user's role. This allows for atomic and efficient role checks using `exists(/professor_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` in security rules, fulfilling the 'Existence over Content' mandate.

This design significantly supports **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** for `list` operations:
*   **Courses:** Professors can list their courses using a query like `where('professorId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. Students can query `/course_enrollments` for their `studentId`, then use the `courseId` values to fetch the courses they are enrolled in, rather than filtering a large `courses` collection.
*   **Assignments, Submissions, Evaluations, Course Content:** All these documents under `/courses/{courseId}/...` paths contain the denormalized `professorId`. Professors can directly query for all such items within *their* courses. Students can query for these within courses they are enrolled in, and for their specific submissions (`submitterId == request.auth.uid`).
*   **Private Messages:** The `/private_messages` collection can be queried by `senderId` or `receiverId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

By embedding all necessary authorization context directly within the documents and using dedicated role collections, security rules become extremely simple and efficient, typically involving only `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` or `exists(/role_collection/$(request.auth.uid))` checks, thus minimizing complexity and maximizing debuggability.
